title: Filesystem Operations
description: File and folder CRUD: create, read, update, delete, move, copy via Puter's layered filesystem module

actors.name: Client
actors.description: HTTP API consumer (browser/app) that calls filesystem REST endpoints

actors.name: System/Batch
actors.description: Internal or batch caller invoking multiple filesystem ops in one request

actors.name: S3 Storage
actors.description: AWS S3 or compatible object store holding binary file data

actors.name: Database
actors.description: Relational DB (fsentries table) holding file metadata, paths, UUIDs, ownership

use_cases.name: Write File
use_cases.description: Upload/overwrite a file's binary content and metadata

use_cases.name: Read File
use_cases.description: Download file binary stream by path or UID

use_cases.name: Read File via Token
use_cases.description: Download file using a file_request_token (unauthenticated access)

use_cases.name: Make Directory
use_cases.description: Create a new directory entry (no S3 object needed)

use_cases.name: Delete File or Folder
use_cases.description: Remove file/folder metadata from DB and binary from S3

use_cases.name: Stat (Get File Info)
use_cases.description: Retrieve metadata for a file/folder (size, dates, UUID, path, etc.)

use_cases.name: Touch File
use_cases.description: Update accessed/modified timestamps of a file

use_cases.name: List Directory
use_cases.description: List entries within a directory (with optional thumbnails)

use_cases.name: List Directory with Subdomains
use_cases.description: List directory contents with subdomain association data

use_cases.name: Copy File or Folder
use_cases.description: Copy file/folder to a destination; uses simple or multipart S3 copy depending on size (<4GB simple, ≥4GB multipart)

use_cases.name: Move File or Folder
use_cases.description: Relocate a file/folder by updating its parent_uid in DB (no S3 change for files; S3 copy+delete for cross-bucket)

use_cases.name: Rename
use_cases.description: Rename a file/folder in place (update name field)

use_cases.name: Search Files
use_cases.description: Query filesystem entries by name pattern

use_cases.name: Update File Metadata
use_cases.description: Update arbitrary metadata fields (sort_by, sort_order, layout, metadata, immutable, etc.)

use_cases.name: Batch Operations
use_cases.description: Execute multiple filesystem operations (write, mkdir, delete) atomically in a single request

use_cases.name: Check Disk Usage
use_cases.description: Return used/available space for the user's filesystem

key_components.name: FilesystemAPIService
key_components.role: Route registrar — mounts all filesystem HTTP endpoints on express app via __on_install.routes()

key_components.name: FSEntryController
key_components.role: Database metadata CRUD + queue-based batch writer for fsentries table
key_components.queue_mechanism: Operations queued as Insert/Update/Delete instances, batch-written via db.batch_write(). Two states: currentState and deferredState (STATUS_READY / STATUS_RUNNING_JOB).

key_components.name: ProxyStorageController
key_components.role: Delegate wrapper for any StorageController implementation (currently delegates to S3StorageController)

key_components.name: S3StorageController
key_components.role: Binary file storage using AWS S3 SDK
public_methods: upload({ uid, file, storage_meta, storage_api }) — PutObject for buffers, multipart for streams
copy({ src_node, dst_storage, storage_api }) — CopyObject (<4GB) or multipart UploadPartCopy (≥4GB)
read({ location, range, version_id }) — GetObjectCommand → Readable stream

key_components.name: PuterFSProvider
key_components.role: Orchestrates high-level FS operations; integrates ACL, metering, events, mountpoint resolution

key_relationships: FilesystemAPIService registers_routes_for all filesystem use cases
important_notes: FSEntryController uses two-queue (current/deferred) batching for all DB writes to minimize round-trips
S3StorageController uses multipart upload (5MB parts, max 4 concurrent) for streaming files
Copy path branches: simple CopyObject for files <4GB, multipart UploadPartCopy (4GiB parts) for larger files
fsentries table columns: id, uuid, parent_uid, name, is_dir, is_public, is_shortcut, is_symlink, size, path, user_id, bucket, bucket_region, created, modified, accessed, metadata, immutable
All write operations (insert/update/delete) go through enqueue_() and are executed as a batch via db.batch_write()
v1 routes (/writeFile, /file) coexist with v2 routes for backward compatibility
PuterFSProvider enforces ACL checks before delegating to FSEntryController/StorageController
